content.problemStatement:
TaskGiven a binary tree, complete the function preOrderTraversal to print the Preorder traversal of the tree.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the binary tree.
Next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain three space separated characters pi,ci,Rip_i, c_i, R_ipi​,ci​,Ri​, describing the tree with edge informations - pip_ipi​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, cic_ici​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if cic_ici​ is left child of pip_ipi​, else `R`.


Output Format
Output on the single line, NNN space separated integers - the preorder traversal of the given tree.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the binary tree. The next N-1 lines contain three space-separated values: p, c, and R, where p is the parent node, c is the child node, and R is either 'L' (c is the left child of p) or 'R' (c is the right child of p).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line with N space-separated integers – the preorder traversal of the given tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Root is 1. Preorder visits 1, then left subtree (2), then right subtree (3) whose left child is 4 and right child is 5.
A tree with a single node (value 1) yields preorder "1".
Root 1, left subtree 2 (with children 4 and 5), then right subtree 3 (with children 6 and 7).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first construct the binary tree based on the given edges. Then, we perform a preorder traversal of the tree, which visits the root node first, then recursively traverses the left subtree, and finally the right subtree. We use a stack to implement the preorder traversal iteratively.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in the tree. The time complexity is linear with respect to the number of nodes in the tree because we visit each node exactly once during the preorder traversal.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the stack used for the preorder traversal, which in the worst case can contain all nodes of the tree.
